commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=056b5787aa88454abd19e31a6824a7904f589666)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an Actions cache for Linux swiftly state, centralizes the Swift version in workflow env, and skips swiftly bootstrap download when cached state is restored.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: restoring `~/.local/share/swiftly` makes swiftly treat the requested version as already installed, and upstream swiftly returns before emitting the post-install prerequisite script in that case. I did not run a real warm GitHub cache because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor warm-cache proof and maintainer review of the workflow repair; ClawSweeper should not auto-repair while real behavior proof is still insufficient.

target.title: ci: cache Linux Swift toolchains
target.body:
## Summary
- cache the Linux swiftly home/toolchain state used by the `build-linux-cli` matrix
- centralize the Swift version in workflow `env.SWIFT_VERSION`
- keep the swiftly bootstrap download/init on every run, so warm cache runs do not execute a cached bootstrap binary
- print the Actions cache-hit value in the Swift install step for easier CI diagnosis

## Why
Every PR runs the Linux x64 and arm64 CLI jobs, and each job currently installs Swift 6.2.1 through swiftly before building/testing the CLI. Caching `~/.local/share/swiftly` lets warm runs reuse the Swift toolchain state while keeping the existing `swiftly install ... --use` command as the correctness gate.

This does not change test coverage or branch-protection behavior. Cold runs still follow the existing download/init/install path. Warm runs still fetch the official swiftly bootstrap, then let swiftly reuse the cached toolchain state.

## Validation
- workflow YAML parsed with `yaml.safe_load`
- checked the `build-linux-cli` job contains `actions/cache@v4` with an OS/arch/Swift-version scoped key
- checked the cache path is limited to `~/.local/share/swiftly`
- extracted the embedded Swift install shell step and ran `bash -n`
- `git diff --check`

## GitHub Actions proof
Previous run for the first cache version: https://github.com/steipete/CodexBar/actions/runs/27857327186

That run proved the cache step did not break either Linux architecture:
- `build-linux-cli (linux-x64, ubuntu-24.04)`: success
  - restore cache, Swift install, release build, Linux tests, and CLI smoke test all succeeded
- `build-linux-cli (linux-arm64, ubuntu-24.04-arm)`: success
  - restore cache, Swift install, release build, Linux tests, and CLI smoke test all succeeded

This updated version is more conservative than the first run: it no longer caches or executes `~/.local/bin/swiftly`; it only caches `~/.local/share/swiftly` and always downloads the official swiftly bootstrap before running `swiftly install`.

A fresh CI run for commit `9281e253` is pending and should provide the final after-change proof for this safer cache boundary.
target.author: yuxin-qiao
